summary:
Claim: It is not true that Zika virus is not transmitted from mother to newborn.  
Evidence: - Zika can be transmitted vertically from mother to fetus, leading to congenital infections. - Global health organizations (CDC, WHO) confirm maternal-fetal transmission as a recognized risk.  
Answer: C
formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Claim: It is not true that Zika virus is not transmitted from mother to newborn.  
Evidence: - Zika can be transmitted vertically from mother to fetus, leading to congenital infections. - Global health organizations (CDC, WHO) confirm maternal-fetal transmission as a recognized risk.  
Answer: C. Answer choice: C
